body:
## Summary
`computeTotal` called `Array.prototype.reduce((a,b)=>a+b)` with no initial value, which throws `TypeError: Reduce of empty array with no initial value` (and yields `NaN` in non-throwing JS contexts) on an empty cart. Seeding the reduce with `0` makes an empty cart total `0` while leaving non-empty carts unchanged (0 is the additive identity).

Closes #1.

## Test plan
Automated via the existing harness `test.js` (`node test.js`, Node `assert`):
- `assert.strictEqual(computeTotal([{price:2,qty:3},{price:5,qty:1}]), 11)` — non-empty unchanged.
- `assert.strictEqual(computeTotal([]), 0)` — empty-cart case (the bug).

Before the fix `node test.js` threw; after the fix it prints `all cart tests passed` and exits 0. Verified green on this branch.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(cart): return 0 for empty cart total
